title: Bed Turnover And Cleaning Dispatch
description: A hospital-operations and compliance workflow that enforces control checks while allocating operational resources and closing governance loops. Focus case: bed turnover and cleaning dispatch.
assumptions: Shared physical resources (carts, kits, audit tablets) are single-instance resources and must be explicitly reserved and released.
EVS technicians and supervisory staff maintain up-to-date availability status in the dispatch system.
Cleaning protocols and checklists fully represent compliance requirements and are enforced by supervisory sign-off steps.
Optional specialty devices (e.g., UV units) are treated as scarce resources and require explicit allocation when used.
risks: Resource contention (insufficient EVS technicians or carts) can delay turnover and breach service-level targets.
Incomplete or skipped checklist steps due to missing inspection forms or audit devices may cause an undetected non-compliant turnover.
Failure to release resources (cart, kit, technician) on completion can reduce capacity for subsequent turnovers.
Use of optional specialty devices without scheduled availability could block other critical workflows and create scheduling conflicts.
